title: Marine Parks and Reserves (Prohibited and Regulated Activities) Regulations, 2006
title_en: Marine Parks and Reserves (Prohibited and Regulated Activities) Regulations, 2006

provisions.title: Citation and date of commencement
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Marine Parks and Reserves (Prohibited and Regulated Activities) Regulations, 2006 and shall come into operation on the date of publication.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these Regulations— “ Act ” mean the Marine Parks and Reserves Act ; “ chemically polluted ” means mixed with an industrial or consumer chemical or chemical product in any form, whether manufactured or obtained from nature; “ coral ” means living or dead calcareous skeleton secreted by marine polyps coelenterates of class anthrozoa deposited in various forms; “ core zone ” means a no-take zone area within the marine park designated as such by the Minister in the zoning scheme regulations made under the Act ; “ general use zone ” means an area within the marine park which is not a core zone or a specified use zone ; “ harpoon ” means a spear-like gun with a rope attached for catching whales or fish; “ high water mark ” means the coast baseline drawn from an average high tide elevation as marked on large scale charts maintained by the Warden and officially recognized by the Government; “ industrial development ” means manufacturing or production and establishment other than domestic; “ MBREMP ” means Mnazi Bay - Ruvuma Estuary Marine Park; “ MIMP ” means the Mafia Island Marine Park so declared under section 9 of the Act ; “ MPA ” Marine Protected Area; “ National Environment Management Council ” means the Council established by the Environmental Management Act , for the oversight of environmental matters in Mainland Tanzania; [ Act No. 20 of 2004 ] “ neap tide ” means a tide having the lowest difference between high and low water levels and occurring at the first and third quarters of the moon; “ port development ” means construction or upgrading of a harbour at which ships take on or offload cargo; “ pull net ” means a fishing net or gear made of thread or wire or any other material designed to be used for the purpose of collecting, capturing, gathering or killing of aquatic flora and fauna by pulling ground ropes; “ resident ” means a person ordinarily resident in the marine park; “ scuba ” means self contained underwater breathing apparatus; “ likembe ” means a net made of nylon twine with 36 plies and mesh size of 6 inches and above; “ spear gun ” means a gun which discharges a missile like spear or any other similar locally made equipment; “ specified use zone ” means an area within the marine park designated as such by the Minister in the zoning scheme regulations made under the Act ; “ stakeholder ” means a person or institution having an interest in or is affected by the activities or proceedings conducted in the marine park under the Act ; “ Warden ” means a person having charge and overall management and responsibility of a marine park under the Act ; Part II – Prohibited activities

provisions.title: Prohibited activities
provisions.content: No person shall within the MMP, MBREMP carry on any of the activities specified in this regulation, namely— (a) the extraction of living resources— (i) by use of beach-seine nets known locally as juya, juya la kojani, kavogo, juya la kusini and kokoro; (ii) subject to the provisions of regulation 5 , by use of any pull nets with stretched-meshsize of less than 2.5 inches, including use of nets known locally as tandilo and mtando; (iii) by using monofilaments locally known as likembe ; (iv) by using a purseine (fishing) net (nyavu za dagaa) nocturnally with light attraction subject to the provisions of regulation 5 ; (v) through any process involving mechanical damage to, or breakage of corals or other benthic habitat or organisms, whether by hand or by use of poles or other implements; (vi) by using explosives; (vii) by trawling; (viii) by using any spear-gun or harpoon propelled in any way except by hand, other than octopus fishing; (ix) by using chemicals or poisons; (x) by using scuba other than for research purposes specifically approved by the Warden ; (xi) involving the deliberate killing of dugong, dolphin and turtles, including removal of turtle eggs, birds, hippos, crocodiles or any terrestrial animals; and (xii) by using fishing gear or methods similar to those described in this sub regulation. (b) production of salt by heating sea water with firewood or other hydrocarbon-based fuel; (c) construction and development in the nature of: (i) port development or dredging; (ii) industrial development ; (iii) use of explosives for any purpose; (iv) dumping into marine waters of solid waste or chemically; and (v) polluted water or liquid. (d) tourist activities involving jet skiing and use of sea planes; (e) any cultivation or farming within the islands of namponda, membelwa/mmongo and bahasha; (f) animal feeding in mangrove forests and catchment areas within the marine park; (g) the burning of lime by use of snail shells locally known as tondo; and (h) notwithstanding the provisions of paragraph (a) to (h) , the Warden incharge may issue a permit after consultation with the village council.

provisions.title: Coral mining
provisions.content: No person shall engage in the mining of coral or coral rock, whether live or dead, below the high water mark . Part III – Regulated activities

provisions.title: Regulated activities
provisions.content: The activities provided for under this Part shall be regulated within the marine parks in the manner and extent indicated herein.

provisions.title: Net fishing of dagaa by local residents
provisions.content: (1) Notwithstanding the provisions of paragraph (iii) of sub regulation (a) of regulation 3 , it shall be lawful for a local resident to engage in the fishing of dagaa with nets in a general zone where waters exceed twenty meters in depth. (2) Notwithstanding the provision of paragraph (b) of sub regulation (1) of regulation 3 , and subject to an approval by the relevant village council, it shall be lawful for a local resident woman scooping small crustaceous "kutanda uduvi and dagaa" in areas of shallow waters.

provisions.title: Sports fishing
provisions.content: (1) No person shall engage in sports fishing within the marine park except in general use zones, having a sports fishing licence issued under the Fisheries Act , 2003 or its regulations; and after obtaining a sports fishing permit from the Warden under these Regulations. (2) Any person permitted to engage in sports fishing pursuant to sub regulation (1) , shall immediately release into the sea any live fish lifted in weight range specified for the species as indicated in the sports fishing permit issued to him. (3) The form of sports fishing permit from the Warden shall be as set-out in the Schedule to these Regulations.

provisions.title: Octopus and sea cucumber fishing
provisions.content: (1) No person shall fish octopus or sea cucumber by skin diving during neap tides. (2) No person shall lift, and no purchaser shall purchase any octopus weighing less than 0.5 kilogram or sea cucumber of less than 18cms. (3) The form of permit for non- resident to fish, octopus or sea cucumber under this regulation shall be as set out Schedule to these Regulations.

provisions.title: Spine lobsters and sea cucumber fishing
provisions.content: (1) No person shall lift or buy a spine lobster of weight below 0.5 kilogram. (2) No person shall lift or buy a spine lobster which have laid eggs as may be visible on the belly. (3) the form of permit for non-residents to fish spine lobster which have laid eggs, and sea cucumber shall be as set out in the Schedule to these Regulations

provisions.title: Land based fossil coral mining and collection of cowries
provisions.content: (1) Mining of fossil corals above the high water mark shall, for purpose of construction and lime-making, be permitted if— (a) the mining is undertaken by a resident within a general-use zone; and (b) a permit has been issued by the Warden for that purpose. (2) The Warden or his designated officer shall specifically approve every proposed mining site after consultation with the relevant village council. (3) No person other than a local resident shall acquire or gather cowries for trade in a general use zone , save with a permit granted for that purpose by the Warden . (4) A permit granted under sub regulation (1) shall be in the form set out in the Schedule to these Regulations.

provisions.title: Mud or sand
provisions.content: Extraction of mud or sand beyond sixty meters from the high water mark of general use zone for the purpose of making mud-bricks or other construction may only be undertaken by a resident .

provisions.title: Mangrove cutting
provisions.content: (1) No person shall cut any mangrove trees except where— (a) the trees are located in a general-use zone or ecologically stable; and (b) the cutting is undertaken by a resident ; (c) resulting poles are used for the construction of a domestic house, communal village building; boat or other structure within a marine park village and shall not be sold to any person or used outside the marine park; (d) purpose of the cutting is to clear access to small areas of beach or inter-tidal areas for a lawful activity; and (e) a permit has been issued for that purpose, by the Warden . (2) The Warden shall, in consultation with the relevant officers, approve all mangrove-cutting sites after consultation with the relevant village. (3) A permit-holder shall not remove any mangrove tree or part of such a tree from any location other than the location specified in the permit issued by the Warden . (4) A permit-holder shall not remove any mangrove tree or part of such a tree in excess of the number of trees specified on the permit issued by the Warden . (5) The Warden may issue or refuse to issue a permit for mangrove cutting, but he shall be guided by the following criteria, namely— (a) whether the specified location is generally suitable having regard, among other things, its conservation status under the applicable zoning plan or not the additional environment impact to the harvested area is in his opinion acceptable; (b) whether or not the applicant has received a permit in the recent past; (c) whether or not the applicant has recently planted or is committed to plant trees within his village area that will provide building poles in the future; (d) collection of dead mangrove branches, fruits, leaves and seeds/outside core zones shall not be restricted and shall not require any permit. (6) Collection of dead mangrove branches, fruits, leaves and seeds outside core zones shall not be restricted. (7) The form of permit under this regulation shall be as set out in the Schedule to these Regulations.

provisions.title: Non-mangrove forest products
provisions.content: (1) No person shall engage in pole-cutting of non-mangrove products except— (a) a resident in a general use zone , for domestic construction; or (b) a resident with a permit in a specified use zone , for domestic construction. (c) a resident in a general use zone , for domestic construction; or (d) a resident with a permit in a specified use zone , for domestic construction. (2) Collection of firewood, raffia fibre (ukindu) in a general use zone or specified use zone shall not be restricted. (3) No person shall light grass fires or otherwise burn any forest products in the marine park, save for making charcoal after obtaining a permit issued by the Warden , provided that no charcoal making shall be permitted in water catchment areas. (4) A permit issued under this regulation shall be as set out in the Schedule to these Regulations.

provisions.title: Salt making
provisions.content: (1) No person shall, other than a resident shall engage in a production of salt by use of solar energy within a general use zone in accordance with a permit issued by the Warden . (2) The Warden or authorized officer shall specify in every permit issued under this regulation, the location approved for the production of salt by use of solar energy. (3) A permit issued under this regulation shall be as set out in the Schedule to these Regulations.

provisions.title: Construction
provisions.content: (1) No person shall establish any large scale processing plant or installation, whether floating, underwater or land based in any area within the: MBREMP or MRs without the consent of the relevant management and properly instituted Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA). (2) Every development of a small scale processing plant or installation, including a desalination plant and processing plant for marine products or otherwise, whether floating or underwater, shall undergo an Environmental Impact Assessment scrutiny in accordance with the Environmental Management Act , 2004. [ Act No. 20 of 2004 ] (3) All new and existing constructions, including tourist lodges and other commercial outlets, jetties and mooring installations, excluding residential houses and public amenities, shall be subject to scrutiny in terms of the environmental impact assessment. (4) Any existing construction or structure, which has not been subjected to the scrutiny regarding by the Environmental Impact Assessment, shall be liable to such scrutiny, and the owner of such structure shall apply the necessary adjustments accordingly.

provisions.title: Scientific research
provisions.content: (1) No person shall undertake any scientific research within any part of the marine park, save with the written permission of the Warden concerned and in consultation with the relevant village council, relevant research institutions and on such terms and conditions as he may attach. (2) Every person who is permitted to undertake research under this regulation shall pay such entry fee as may be prescribed, save under the Warden ’s exemption, if in his opinion the research will be of substantial benefit to the marine park. (3) The form of application for a grant of permit for scientific research under this regulation shall be as set out in the Schedule to these Regulations. (4) Report on any research findings under sub-regulation (1) shall be availed to the Warden .

provisions.title: Permits and licences
provisions.content: (1) The Warden may issue a permit or license for the purposes of these Regulations, subject to such conditions and directions, as he may deem necessary, but he shall give his reasons in writing for his refusal to issue a permit or license requested by an applicant. (2) The permits and licences issued under sub-regulation (1) , shall be as set out in the Schedule to these Regulations. (3) A permit or licence issued under this regulation shall remain the property of the marine park and be produced on demand to the marine park officials.

provisions.title: Offence and penalties
provisions.content: (1) Any person who contravenes the provisions of these Regulations or any direction made hereunder commits an offense, and on conviction shall be liable to a fine of not less than fifty thousand shillings or imprisonment for a term of not less than six months, or to both. (2) Any object, instrument, product or thing used in the breach of these Regulations shall be liable to forfeiture.

provisions.title: Powers of arrest
provisions.content: In addition to the marine parks and reserves officials, members of marine park villages appointed in writing for general or specific cases by the relevant village councils and approved by the Warden , may exercise powers of arrest and other enforcement functions under the Act as may be determined by him in respect of activities carried on within the marine park.

provisions.title: Honorary rangers
provisions.content: (1) The Unit Manager or the Warden may issue distinctive identity cards to honorary rangers among members of relevant villages who shall be charged with patrol duties and other enforcement operations pertaining to the laws and regulations governing marine parks and reserves. (2) The form of identity cards for enforcement officers of marine parks and reserves shall be as set out in the Schedule to these Regulations. Schedule (J) General form of application for the grant of a permit/licence/authorisation Under Mnazi Bay - Ruvuma Estuary Marine Park (Prohibited and regulated activities) Form No. MPR 2 Form No. MPR 3 Form No. MPR 4 History of this document 15 September 2006 this version Published in Gazette of the United Republic of Tanzania 37 Commenced 04 September 2006 Assented to Related documents More documents like this one [] [] [] [] ▲ To the top > Contact us Visit our Twitter/X profile Visit our Linked In profile Visit our YouTube channel About TanzLII TanzLII is operated by the Judiciary of Tanzania and the Office of the Attorney General, Chief Parliamentary Draftsman, and publishes the law of Tanzania for free online access for all.
